title: Service standards - Canada.ca
content:
The CRA has 47 service standards for 2016-2017 which demonstrate our commitment to taxpayers and benefit recipients and outline the services they should expect under normal circumstances.

### Here is a detailed list of our service standards, as well as targets, for each service standard:
Tax returns, benefits, and claims
Processing T1 individual income tax returns (EFILE and NETFILE)
Our goal is to process an electronic T1 return (EFILE and NETFILE) within an average of two weeks of receipt
Processing T1 individual income tax returns (paper)
Our goal is to process a paper T1 return and mail a notice of assessment, and refund, if applicable, in an average of four to six weeks of receipt
Processing T2 corporation income tax returns – electronic
Our goal is to process an electronic T2 return (corporation income tax) within 45 calendar days of receipt
Processing T2 corporation income tax returns – paper
Our goal is to process a paper T2 return (corporation income tax) within 90 calendar days of receipt
Processing GST/HST electronic returns
Our goal is to process an electronic GST/HST return within 30 calendar days of receipt
Processing GST/HST paper returns
Our goal is to process a paper GST/HST return within 60 calendar days of receipt
Processing T3 trust returns
Our goal is to process a T3 trust return within four months of receipt
Processing excise tax, excise duty, softwood lumber returns, and air travellers’ security charge returns
Our goal is to process an excise tax, an excise duty, a softwood lumber, or an air travellers’ security charge return within 60 calendar days of receipt
Responding to taxpayer-requested adjustments (T1)
Our goal is to mail a notice of reassessment within an average of eight weeks of receipt of all requisite information from the taxpayer
Responding to taxpayer-requested adjustments (T1) received by Internet
Our goal is to mail a notice of reassessment within an average of two weeks of receipt of all requisite information from the taxpayer
Responding to benefit and credit enquiries – timeliness
Our goal is to respond to a written enquiry or a telephone referral from a call centre within 80 calendar days of receipt
Responding to benefit and credit enquiries – accuracy
Our goal is to respond with correct information to a written enquiry or telephone referral from a call centre, to accurately process new recipient information, and to accurately issue a payment, notice, or letter
Processing benefit applications – accuracy
Our goal is to accurately process the appropriate payment and notice, or send a letter requesting additional information
Processing benefit applications – timeliness
Our goal is to send a payment, notice, or explanation within 80 calendar days of receipt
Claims – SR&ED tax incentives – claimant-requested adjustments to non-refundable claims
Our goal is to process claims for tax incentives from businesses that conduct scientific research and experimental development ( SR&ED ) in Canada within 365 calendar days from receipt of a complete claim for non-refundable claims related to adjustments requested to previously filed income tax returns
Claims – SR&ED tax incentives – claimant-requested adjustments to refundable claims
Our goal is to process claims for tax incentives from businesses that conduct scientific research and experimental development (SR&ED) in Canada within 240 calendar days from receipt of a complete claim, for refundable claims related to adjustments requested to previously filed income tax returns
Claims – SR&ED tax incentives – non-refundable claims
Our goal is to process claims for tax incentives from businesses that conduct scientific research and experimental development (SR&ED) in Canada within 365 calendar days from receipt of a complete claim for non-refundable claims
Claims – SR&ED tax incentives – refundable claims
Our goal is to process claims for tax incentives from businesses that conduct scientific research and experimental development (SR&ED) in Canada within 120 calendar days from receipt of a complete claim for refundable claims
Claims – video and film tax credits – refundable claims – audited
Our goal is to review T2 corporation income tax returns that include claims for the Canadian Film or Video Production Tax Credit, the Film or Video Production Services Tax Credit, the B.C. Film and Television Tax credit, the B.C. Production Services Tax Credit, the Manitoba Film and Video Production Tax Credit, and the Ontario Film and Television Tax Credit, and the Ontario Production Services Tax Credit within 120 calendar days from the date of receipt where audit action is undertaken
Claims – video and film tax credits – refundable claims – unaudited
Our goal is to review T2 corporation income tax returns that include claims for the Canadian Film or Video Production Tax Credit, the Film or Video Production Services Tax Credit, the B.C. Film and Television Tax credit, the B.C. Production Services Tax Credit, the Manitoba Film and Video Production Tax Credit, and the Ontario Film and Television Tax Credit, and the Ontario Production Services Tax Credit within 60 calendar days from the date of receipt where no audit action is undertaken
Pension, deferred, and education plans
Deferred income plans – response to written enquiries
Our goal is to respond to a written enquiry within 60 calendar days
Actuarial valuation reports
Our goal is to provide a decision on the recommended employer contributions to a registered pension plan, within 270 calendar days of receipt of the actuarial valuation report
Applications to register pension plans
Our goal is to provide a decision within 180 calendar days of receipt of an application to register a pension plan
Deferred profit sharing plans – reviewing applications
Our goal is to provide a decision within 180 calendar days of receipt of an application to register a deferred profit-sharing plan
Education savings plans (applications to register, amend, or terminate)
Our goal is to provide a decision within 60 calendar days to a request to approve, amend, or terminate a specimen for an education savings plan
Retirement income funds (applications to register, amend, or terminate)
Our goal is to provide a decision within 60 calendar days to a request to approve, amend, or terminate a specimen for a retirement income fund
Retirement savings plans (applications to register, amend, or terminate)
Our goal is to provide a decision within 60 calendar days to a request to approve, amend, or terminate a specimen for a retirement savings plan
Charities – responding to telephone enquiries
Our goal is to respond to a call in the agent queue within two minutes
Charities – written enquiries – routine
Our goal is to review and respond to a routine Footnote 8 written enquiry within 30 calendar days
Charities – written enquiries – complex
Our goal is to review and respond to a complex Footnote 9 written enquiry within 75 calendar days
Charities – responding to simple applications
Our goal is to respond to a complete simple Footnote 11 application for charitable registration within two months
Charities – responding to regular applications
Our goal is to respond to a complete regular Footnote 12 application for charitable registration within six months
Rulings and interpretations
GST/HST rulings and interpretations – telephone enquiries
Our goal is to respond to a call in the agent queue within two minutes
GST/HST rulings and interpretations – written enquiries
Our goal is to respond to written requests for GST/HST rulings and interpretations within 45 business days of CRA receipt of the request and all the relevant facts and supporting documentation. This excludes highly technical and precedent and/or policy-setting rulings and interpretations
Advance income tax rulings
Our goal is to issue an advance income tax ruling within 90 business days of receipt of all essential information from the client
Technical interpretations
Our goal is to issue a technical interpretation within 90 business days of receipt of all essential information from the client
Taxpayer relief requests – 30 day acknowledgment
Our goal is to acknowledge receipt of a taxpayer relief request to cancel or waive penalties and interest within 30 calendar days of receipt
First contact letter for disputes
Our goal is to acknowledge a taxpayer dispute within 30 calendar days of receipt
Service Complaints – two-day acknowledgement
Our goal is to acknowledge receipt of the complaint within two business days
Service Complaints – 30 day resolution
Our goal is to resolve the complaint within 30 business days
Enquiries and account updates
e-Services Helpdesk – telephone service level
Our goal is to respond to a call in the agent queue within two minutes
General enquiries – telephone service level
Our goal is to respond to a call in the agent queue within two minutes
Canada child benefit enquiries – telephone service level
Our goal is to respond to a call in the agent queue within two minutes
GST/HST credit enquiries telephone service level
Our goal is to respond to a call in the agent queue within two minutes
Business enquiries – telephone service level
Our goal is to respond to a call in the agent queue within two minutes
Processing a request to authorize or cancel a representative – paper
Our goal is to process your complete paper request to authorize or cancel a representative within 20 business days of receipt by the CRA, provided the request is complete
Processing a request to authorize or cancel a representative – electronic
Our goal is to process your complete electronic request to authorize or cancel a representative within five business days of receipt by the CRA, provided the request is complete

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
